evals.name: first-time-install-defaults
evals.prompt: i wanna install that append-ryan task on my mac. just give me the defaults — downloads folder, 7am every morning, no email for now. lets just get it running
evals.expected_output: Skill walks user through minimal setup (confirm defaults), copies scripts into ~/Documents/Claude/Scheduled/append-ryan/scripts/, writes email-config.json with enabled:false, registers the scheduled task with cron '0 7 * * *' and the runtime prompt template populated with the default paths.

evals.name: install-with-email
evals.prompt: set up the append ryan thing. i want it to email me drafts when a section fills up. send to soren@vibeos.com from my gmail at soren.vale@gmail.com, also cc rod@catomtrucking.com. fire at 6am. assume i already have the gmail mcp installed
evals.expected_output: Skill collects email config (from, to, cc), writes email-config.json with enabled:true, copies scripts, registers scheduled task with cron '0 6 * * *' and the runtime prompt populated for email-enabled mode (mentions gmail draft step, references the email-config.json path).

evals.name: manual-run-now
evals.prompt: how do i run the append ryan task right now without waiting til tomorrow morning? want to test it before i leave the office
evals.expected_output: Skill explains either (a) Cowork's 'Run now' button on the scheduled task, or (b) running the run_daily_append.py script directly with explicit args. Doesn't reinstall anything. Probably mentions the optional --dry-run flag.
